perturbation.original_text: c. “Customers” or “Customer” shall mean any Person to whom a Banking Organization rendered or provided Banking and Financial Services at any time during the one-year period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with a Financial Institution: (i) with whom Employee had any material contact (either directly or indirectly); (ii) which Employee managed, had responsibility for or regularly serviced while employed by the Financial Institution, provided, however, this shall not be construed to include supervising those who have such a relationship to the Customer; or, (iii) about which Employee obtained Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: c. “Customers” or “Customer” shall mean any Person to whom a Banking Organization rendered or provided Banking and Financial Services at any time during the one-year period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with a Financial Institution: (i) with whom Employee had any material contact (either directly or indirectly); (ii) which Employee managed, had responsibility for or regularly serviced while employed by the Financial Institution, provided, however, this *shall* be construed to include supervising those who have such a relationship to the Customer; or, (iii) about which Employee obtained Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not' to 'shall' in the definition of 'Customers,' we introduce an ambiguity regarding whether supervising relationships with customers constitutes 'having material contact' for the purposes of non-solicitation. This change creates a contradiction with the original intent, creating in text contradiction regarding the extent of customer contact.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c. “Customers” or “Customer” shall mean any Person to whom a Banking Organization rendered or provided Banking and Financial Services at any time during the one-year period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with a Financial Institution: (i) with whom Employee had any material contact (either directly or indirectly); (ii) which Employee managed, had responsibility for or regularly serviced while employed by the Financial Institution, provided, however, this shall be construed to include supervising those who have such a relationship to the Customer; or, (iii) about which Employee obtained Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Customers' by changing 'shall not' to 'shall' regarding the inclusion of supervising customer relationships. This directly contradicts the initial intention of excluding supervisors from being considered as having 'material contact' with customers. This alteration introduces ambiguity and inconsistency regarding the scope of 'customer contact,' creating a strong in-text contradiction within the document as it pertains to non-solicitation obligations. The changed text and contradicted text appear to be in the same location, but the change itself causes the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: d. “Employees,” “Agents,” and “Independent Contractors” shall mean any and all persons employed or contracted by a Banking Organization or who were employed or contracted by a Banking Organization, as an employee, agent or independent contractor, within the six (6) month period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with either Financial Institution.
perturbation.changed_text: d. “Employees,” “Agents,” and “Independent Contractors” shall mean only those persons directly employed by a Banking Organization as a full-time employee within the six (6) month period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with either Financial Institution.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition includes all persons 'employed or contracted' by a banking organization. The revised definition now limits this to only 'full-time employees', which creates an in-text contradiction in interpreting the non-solicitation clauses. It is unclear if non-solicitation applies to those that were 'contracted' as stated in the original text, generating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. Non-Solicitation of Employees and Others . In consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of two (2) years immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: c. Solicit, entice or persuade (or attempt to Solicit, entice or persuade) any Employee to terminate his or her employment with a Banking Organization, or offer or provide employment (whether such employment is with Employee or any other Person) either on a full-time or part-time, consulting or Independent Contractor basis; or d. Solicit, entice or persuade (or attempt to Solicit, entice or persuade) any Independent Contractors or Agents to terminate their contract or relationship with a Banking Organization or discontinue providing services to a Banking Organization and/or Customers; or e. Solicit, entice or persuade (or attempt to Solicit, entice or persuade) any suppliers, vendors or others who were supplying services or goods to a Banking Organization during the one year period prior to Employee’s termination, to terminate, reduce, limit or change their business or relationship with a Banking Organization.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Employees, Agents, and Independent Contractors' broadly. The changed text narrows this definition to only 'full-time employees'. The contradicted text (Section 6) includes clauses about soliciting 'Independent Contractors or Agents'. Because the definition of these terms changed, it's now unclear whether the non-solicitation clause regarding contractors and agents still applies, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5 c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement *is* intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5 c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'is not intended' to 'is intended', we create an ambiguity and in-text contradiction regarding whether the agreement prevents the employee from working for a competitor. This alteration directly conflicts with the later clauses that outline exceptions for supervisory roles within competing organizations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5 c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies "This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers" to "This Agreement is intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers". This change directly contradicts the subsequent clauses that outline exceptions for supervisory roles within competing organizations, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction as the agreement now both allows and disallows employment with competing organizations, according to the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.changed_text: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to consider the potential benefits of terminating, reducing, limiting, or changing the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'terminate, reduce, limit, or change' to 'consider the potential benefits of terminating, reducing, limiting, or changing' in Section 5.b, we introduce ambiguity. The original text strictly prohibits advising customers to change their relationship. The modified text only prohibits advising them to consider it, which can be seen as a direct incitement to change in the relationship, while not being explicit about advising to change it. This creates an in-text contradiction in understanding the extent of the non-solicitation clause and allowing it to be interpreted in favor of the employee.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to consider the potential benefits of terminating, reducing, limiting, or changing the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; 5 c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The original text in 5.b strictly prohibited advising customers to terminate, reduce, limit, or change their relationship. The changed text modifies this to only prohibit advising them to 'consider the potential benefits' of doing so. This introduces ambiguity and a potential loophole, as it allows the employee to indirectly influence a customer's decision to change their relationship, while not being explicitly advising it. This contradicts the overall intent of the non-solicitation clause in Section 5, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the scope of prohibited activities and its enforceability. The definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction applies.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Available Relief . Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein. Accordingly, in the event of a breach or of a threatened or attempted breach by Employee of Sections 4, 5, or 6, in addition to all other remedies to which a Banking Organization is entitled under law, in equity, or otherwise (including, but not limited to, monetary damages), a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order and/or preliminary or permanent injunction (without the necessity of showing any actual damage) or a decree of specific performance and no bond or other security shall be required in that connection.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Available Relief . Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a *material* breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein. Accordingly, in the event of a breach or of a threatened or attempted breach by Employee of Sections 4, 5, or 6, in addition to all other remedies to which a Banking Organization is entitled under law, in equity, or otherwise (including, but not limited to, monetary damages), a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order and/or preliminary or permanent injunction (without the necessity of showing any actual damage) or a decree of specific performance and no bond or other security shall be required in that connection.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'material' before 'breach' in Section 9, we introduce a subjective standard that could be used to challenge injunctive relief. Originally any breach entitled the Banking Organization to certain relief. The amendment would require proof of materiality, potentially contradicting with Section 8's blue pencil provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Available Relief . Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of a material breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein. Accordingly, in the event of a breach or of a threatened or attempted breach by Employee of Sections 4, 5, or 6, in addition to all other remedies to which a Banking Organization is entitled under law, in equity, or otherwise (including, but not limited to, monetary damages), a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order and/or preliminary or permanent injunction (without the necessity of showing any actual damage) or a decree of specific performance and no bond or other security shall be required in that connection.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that any breach of Sections 4, 5, or 6 would entitle the Banking Organization to certain relief. The changed text modifies Section 9 to state that only a 'material' breach would trigger that relief. The contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text, but this is irrelevant as the explanation clearly states the contradiction) now requires proof of materiality. This contradicts the initial intent of 'any breach' being sufficient for relief and introduces ambiguity, as the definition of 'material' is subjective and open to interpretation. This requires a higher threshold to qualify for injunctive relief. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
